content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA consisting of NNN **distinct** integers (1≤Ai≤2⋅N)(1 \le A_i \le 2 \cdot N)(1≤Ai​≤2⋅N), we have to perform KKK moves.
We perform the following steps in one move:


Select an integer XXX such that 1≤X≤2⋅N1 \le X \le 2 \cdot N1≤X≤2⋅N and X≠AiX \ne A_iX=Ai​ for all iii (X(X(X is not equal to any element of the current array))).
Append XXX to AAA.
The score of this move =(max⁡1≤i≤∣A∣Ai)−X= (\max_{1 \le i \le |A|} A_i) - X=(max1≤i≤∣A∣​Ai​)−X.

Note that the score is calculated after appending XXX. Thus, XXX can be the maximum element of the array as well.
For e.g. if A=[3,1,5]A = [3, 1, 5]A=[3,1,5] and we append X=2X = 2X=2 to AAA, then, AAA becomes [3,1,5,2][3, 1, 5, 2][3,1,5,2] and the score of this move is max⁡([3,1,5,2])−2=5−2=3\max([3, 1, 5, 2]) - 2 = 5 - 2 = 3max([3,1,5,2])−2=5−2=3.

Find the **maximum sum** of scores we can achieve after performing **exactly** KKK moves.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the size of the array AAA and the number of moves respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum sum** of scores we can achieve after performing **exactly** KKK moves.

It is guaranteed that it is always possible to perform KKK moves under the given constraints.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: We can perform the following move:

Append X=2X = 2X=2. Score of this move =max⁡([1,3,6,2])−2=6−2=4= \max([1, 3, 6, 2]) - 2 = 6 - 2 = 4=max([1,3,6,2])−2=6−2=4

Hence, the maximum total score is 444.
Test Case 2: We can perform the following moves:

Append X=4X = 4X=4. Score of this move =max⁡([1,2,4])−4=4−4=0= \max([1, 2, 4]) - 4 = 4 - 4 = 0=max([1,2,4])−4=4−4=0
Append X=3X = 3X=3. Score of this move =max⁡([1,2,4,3])−3=4−3=1= \max([1, 2, 4, 3]) - 3 = 4 - 3 = 1=max([1,2,4,3])−3=4−3=1

Hence, the maximum total score is 0+1=10+1=10+1=1.
Test Case 3: We can perform the following moves:

Append X=4X = 4X=4. Score of this move =max⁡([1,2,5,4])−4=5−4=1= \max([1, 2, 5, 4]) - 4 = 5 - 4 = 1=max([1,2,5,4])−4=5−4=1
Append X=3X = 3X=3. Score of this move =max⁡([1,2,5,4,3])−3=5−3=2= \max([1, 2, 5, 4, 3]) - 3 = 5 - 3 = 2=max([1,2,5,4,3])−3=5−3=2

Hence the maximum total score is 1+2=31+2=31+2=3.
